title: Federal appeals court skeptical of cases for and against Trump tariff authority
summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, pictured July 31, 2025. (Photo by Ashley Murray/States Newsroom) WASHINGTON — Judges on the U.S. Appeals Court for the Federal Circuit questioned the legality of President Donald Trump’s sweeping emergency tariffs Thursday as the White House pushes on with its Aug. 1 deadline for import taxes at levels not seen since the 1930s. The case originated from consolidated lawsuits brought by a handful of business owners and a dozen Democratic state attorneys general who argued the president does not have the authority to impose tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, or IEEPA. Through nearly two hours of questioning Thursday, the 11-judge panel probed whether the president could use IEEPA authority to set tariffs without approval from Congress. The U.S. Department of Justice’s Brett Shumate argued the law is “one of the most powerful tools” to protect the economy and national security during emergencies. Oregon So...

title: Months after stroke-like illness, an Alaska state senator is still recovering
summary: Sen. Donny Olson, D-Golovin, is seen in his office within the Alaska Capitol on May 20, 2025. (James Brooks photo/Alaska Beacon) On the last day of Alaska’s legislative session in May, Sen. Donny Olson made a mistake. The longtime Democratic senator from Golovin, mishearing Senate Majority Leader Cathy Giessel, voted to sustain Gov. Mike Dunleavy’s veto of a bill that increases the state’s public school education formula. Heads snapped to Olson, and his colleague, Sen. Lyman Hoffman, D-Bethel, elbowed him.
